The Quiet Library

By Eleanor Ash

A library is a machine for attention. Its long tables and low lamps are not decoration; they are an argument about how a mind should be treated when it is trying to think.

On benches and silence

The best reading rooms refuse the open-plan office's central lie — that proximity is the same as collaboration. Here, proximity is a shared promise to be quiet.

Consider the bench. It seats strangers side by side and asks nothing of them but stillness. That is a remarkable social technology, and we have largely forgotten how to build it.

A room can hold a person more gently than most people can.

So the next time you design a place for thought, begin with the bench and the lamp, and let everything else apologize for the space it takes.
